danger_signals.visual_form: speaker feedback/haptic thumping when helicopter is hit
danger_signals.meaning: player helicopter taking damage; immediate threat feedback

danger_signals.visual_form: targeting reticle behavior (inferred from guided missile mechanics)
danger_signals.meaning: enemy acquisition of player position; imminent attack danger

danger_signals.visual_form: helicopter altitude/pitch deviation (affected by altitude control lever)
danger_signals.meaning: loss of flight control; environmental or combat hazard

safety_signals.visual_form: helicopter responds smoothly to analog collective lever input
safety_signals.meaning: stable flight achieved; safe operational control

safety_signals.visual_form: machine gun, rockets, and guided missiles visibly available in loadout
safety_signals.meaning: player capability to neutralize threats; tactical safety margin

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: AT1196 Steel Talons combat helicopter designation
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: competitive mode shows opponent helicopter as distinct entity to destroy
air-to-surface guided missile targets; ground installations inferred from 19 mission structure

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: shared battlefield with 19 sequential missions; ground targets and air opposition visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: helicopter maintains flight integrity; enemy AI responds to player proximity and weapon targeting

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: two helicopters visible in same airspace; opponent helicopter rendered as distinct adversary entity
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: opponent helicopter actively maneuvering to attack player; mutual threat avoidance and weapon deployment

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: flight model becomes more complex; rudder pedals and analog control have amplified effect on helicopter behavior
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player loses assisted flight stability; pitch, roll, and yaw become more demanding; greater freedom of movement enables tactical advantage

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: guided missile count decreases visibly; machine gun and rockets remain unlimited
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player must rely on basic armament; tactical options reduce as mission progresses

environmental_storytelling.example: 19 sequential missions in cooperative mode establish escalating conflict; early missions likely easier, suggesting military campaign narrative arc
flat-shaded polygon 3D terrain provides visual geography of combat zones; targets positioned in landscape convey operation area and objective locations
machine gun (unlimited), rockets (limited), and air-to-surface guided missiles (very limited) suggest resource scarcity and tactical decision-making about engagement priority

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: responds to joystick input, analog altitude lever, and rudder pedals; maintains 3D position in polygon-rendered airspace
speaker emits thumping feedback; visual frame integrity potentially affected (inferred)
loses assisted flight stability; control inputs produce more dramatic pitch, roll, and yaw changes; greater maneuverability possible
actively maneuvers in 3D space; deploys weapons against player; avoids incoming fire
stationary or moving targets; respond to player weapons with destruction or evasion patterns

notes: Steel Talons is a 1991 combat flight simulator with limited narrative world-state documentation in available sources. Visual communication relies primarily on 3D polygon rendering, physical feedback (arcade seat speaker), and control responsiveness to convey helicopter status and threat conditions. Faction distinctions are minimal; the game presents a binary threat model (player vs. environment/opponents) rather than complex political situations. Genesis port maintains core mechanics but may reduce visual fidelity relative to arcade original.
